transcript.text:
*Tzuyang
*From 'Money Heist'
*Spanish Mukbang Crew
[The following is not a direct translation from the series] It's written in Korean the way it sounds for comedy purposes
My pizza!
*Disappearance of Pizza
It's already gone
*We need to find the culprit
*Her stomach is..
*Limitless
Should we call your master?
Okay
*Someone unimaginable
You took it lightly
You shouldn't have done that
Did you see her?

She's good
She is coming back by midnight for more
What are you talking about?
*She's coming
Run..
Run!
*I want to eat more
She's thinking about cooking more Ramyun noodles
*Tzuyang's Netflix Mukbang List
Hello everyone
As my subscribers recommended, I will be doing Netflix original recommendations
And its mukbang scenes from the series
The first Netflix series I'm going to recommend is
'Street Food' Asia Episode
The reason why I enjoyed this so much is because
of the mother that prepares the food

It really touched my heart
I also thought about wanting to eat the food there too
*Kwangjang Market
I think it's here
*The same location as the series
Hello
This is the place that got featured on 'Street Food' on Netflix
She's the same person from the series
That's really cool
1 plate of Meat Dumplings
1 plate of Kimchi Dumplings
1 bowl of Kalguksu (Noodle Soup)
Thank you
I'll try the dumplings first
It's just a very standard delicious dumpling
Let's try the kimchi one now

The kimchi one tastes great
It goes really well with soy sauce
Thank you
Enjoy~
That looks yummy
There's a lot of squash
You can't forget about the kimchi with the noodle soup
*Watching her
Doesn't she do mukbang?
She's so much prettier in person
How do you know so well?
I've seen it before
Plum juice for you
Really? Thank you
I was just passing by but chose to sit here
Only because I saw Tzuyang

*The person from 'Street Food' Asia Episode
*Scene from 'Street Food'
You finished it all?
Such a lovely lady
That eats so lovely
When did they film for Netflix here?
2 years ago
I honestly didn't know what Netflix was
So I refused at first
And my kid was in shock
Saying it's very famous
*A photo with the owner actress
The next series I want to recommend is
about a famous chef that quits his restaurant to start a food truck business
I really wanted to try the Cuban Sandwich as I was watching this series
*Scene from 'Chef'

*After receiving a bad review from a food critic at his restaurant,
*He quits his restaurant
*And he challenges himself to drive around America with his food truck with his Cuban Sandwich
*Can he bring his reputation back?
It's over there
*Seoripul Food Truck
Hello
Is there a menu?
Oh these 2?
I'll take both
I prepared a beverage just for Tzuyang
It's a gift Thank you
#MiniFuel
Perfect fit
Let's try the original first
Amazing

People usually use the box to avoid the spill
Oh like this!
I took 1 bite and it's so good
There's not much in here but it's really tasty
It's delicious
*How did you start Food Truck business?
I used to be in the food industry
And had an opportunity to take part in 'Youth Food truck'
And I got to meet the famous Baek Jong Won chef
Were there any difficulties starting a Food Truck?
I hated rain at first
The weather has a great effect on the Food Truck business
I'm sure a lot of Food Truck owners will agree with me
Even from the Netflix series 'Chef', the owner had to leave his restaurant to do Food Truck
Just like the owner here
The food was really really tasty

I wish him the best in his future endeavors
Tzuyang Hwaiting!
Ta-da!
I am here at Shindang-dong
I came here because of the food featured on Netflix
*Scene from 'Somebody Feed Phil' Korea Episode
*Shindang-dong Spicy Rice Cake Town
I can already smell delicious food
This place opened in 1972
It's been almost 50 years
I'm very curious to how it tastes like
I'll eat the noodles first
With the glass noodles
Hot
What I learned after watching 'Somebody Feed Phil' is that
Spicy Rice Cake is a very common dish in South Korea

But it's not for people that are not from Korea
I thought it was very cool
Let's get the rice cake and the fish cake
Gotta scoop the egg with the spoon
After splitting it in half
I enjoy eating egg yolk
But I like the egg whites when I eat spicy rice cake
Thanks for the food~ (I had a great meal)
*Butter?
*Corn?
I made some corn this time
It is from my next Netflix recommendation 'The Chef Show'
*Scene from 'The Chef Show'
Everybody knows 'The Lion King', right?
The director of that movie John Favreau fell in love with cooking while he was filming movies
So he cooks with a famous chef

*Scene from 'Lucifer'
Over here
I chose this series because of this specific scene
It is called 'Lucifer'
I was shocked as I was watching this
because I never imagined a combination with potato chips with chocolate
But they made it seem so delicious
And it actually is delicious
You guys can enjoy watching Netflix with your potato chips with Nutella now
Subtitles by Hyun Choi antharoni@gmail.com
